# Schemas reference — every shipped operation, by JSON schema

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by bin/regen-llm-docs from gui/schemas/operations/.
     Edit the.schema.json files and re-run.  This file is complementary
     to the hand-curated unit-ops.md (which has groupings, prose and
     worked examples); this file is the alphabetical schema dump.

     HAND-CURATED ADDITIONS (will be lost on a blind regen until folded in):
       - the `valve`, `electricLoad` and `steamTables` entries (no .schema.json yet);
       - the distillationColumn condenser/reboiler heat-port note.
     When schema files for valve + electricLoad + steamTables land, drop these
     and rerun bin/regen-llm-docs. -->

*32 operations documented (29 carry a JSON schema in `gui/schemas/operations/`;
`valve`, `electricLoad` and `steamTables` are shipped ops whose schema files are
not yet written — their entries below are hand-curated until
`bin/regen-llm-docs` can pick them up).*

## `absorber`  (absorber operation)

Operation block of a counter-current multistage gas absorber (Kremser group method).  Hardware-only: specify the number of theoretical stages (the column height).  The recovery of each solute is a RESULT, computed from the absorption factor A_i = L/(K_i V) and the Kremser equation; a soluble gas with a Henry's-law entry uses K = H(T)/P.  For 'I need 95 % recovery' wrap the unit in a DesignSpec outerDict that manipulates $stages with a target on this unit's recovery_<solute> KPI.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `stages` | ✓ | number | - | Number of equilibrium stages = the column height.  More stages give more recovery (the Kremser chart). |

## `adiabaticFlash`  (adiabaticFlash operation)

Operation block of a two-phase flash with an outer Newton loop on T so that the energy balance closes.  Credo-pure: hardware = outlet pressure only.  An adiabatic flash has Q == 0 by definition; if you want heat addition, chain a `heater` upstream.  T_out, V/F and the regime are RESULTS, not specs.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `P` | ✓ | number | bar | Pressure at which the flash is solved. |

## `bagFilter`  (bagFilter operation)

Fabric (bag-house) filter -- the fine, high-efficiency complement to the cyclone.  Hardware: the cloth area.  Collection is near-total and only mildly size-dependent (the dust cake is the filter); the pressure drop is cake-dominated and a RESULT, ΔP = μ·V·(K1 + K2·W) with V = Q/area the air-to-cloth ratio.  Needs a particle-size distribution on the feed (solids block) and a transport model for the gas viscosity.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `filterArea` | ✓ | number | m2 | Total fabric area [m²].  Sets the face velocity V = Q_gas/A (the air-to-cloth ratio, typically 1-3 m/min). |
| `K1` |   | number | 1/m | Darcy resistance of the bare cloth [1/m] (default 1e9, calibratable). |
| `K2` |   | number | m/kg | Darcy resistance per unit areal dust load [m/kg] (default 1e10, calibratable). |
| `arealDustLoad` |   | number | kg/m2 | Cake mass per unit cloth area [kg/m²] at the chosen point in the cleaning cycle (default 0.5).  Drives the cake pressure drop. |
| `penetration0` |   | number | - | Penetration of the finest particles in η(d)=1-P0·exp(-d/d_c) (default 0.02, calibratable). |
| `dCharacteristic` |   | number | m | Size scale of the grade-efficiency rise [m] (default 1e-6). |

## `bubbleT`  (bubbleT operation)

Bubble-point temperature at fixed pressure: find the T at which the first vapour bubble forms from a saturated liquid of the given composition.  Solved by Newton-1D in T on Sum_i K_i(T) x_i = 1.  Hardware-only: specify the pressure P; the bubble temperature is the RESULT.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `P` | ✓ | number | Pa | System pressure at which the bubble point is evaluated. |

## `compressor`  (compressor operation)

Operation block of a credo-pure gas-phase compressor.  Hardware-only: specify the shaft power W_shaft (W or kW, positive) and the isentropic efficiency eta.  The discharge pressure P_out, the discharge temperature T_out and the compression ratio are RESULTS computed from the isentropic-then-efficiency energy balance.  For 'I need P_out = X' wrap the unit in a DesignSpec outerDict that manipulates $W_shaft with a target on this unit's P_out KPI.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `W_shaft` | ✓ | number | W | Mechanical power delivered to the gas.  Think of it as the motor rating.  Positive (this is a compressor; use a turbine block for expansi… |
| `eta` | ✓ | number | - | Machine characteristic: w_isentropic / w_real.  0 < eta <= 1.  Lower eta means more of the shaft work is dissipated as heat (entropy gene… |

## `crystalliser`  (crystalliser operation)

Crystalliser.  Two `model`s: `equilibrium` (default) is the cooling-yield model --- a solution is cooled to the operating temperature, the solubility falls and the excess crystallises, the mother liquor leaving saturated (yield, dissolved solute and cooling duty Q are RESULTS from the solute's `solubility {}` curve).  `MSMPR` is the continuous mixed-suspension mixed-product-removal crystalliser solved by the method of moments: the kinetics (referenced from constant/crystallisation) set the particle-size distribution, and the steady supersaturation, growth/nucleation rate, dominant crystal size and magma density are RESULTS.  One product: the magma (crystals in the solid phase + mother liquor).

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `operatingTemperature` | ✓ | number | K | The temperature the crystalliser holds (an operating setpoint).  The mother liquor leaves saturated (equilibrium) or supersaturated (MSMP… |
| `volume` |   | number | m3 | Working suspension volume of the continuous vessel (MSMPR model only).  Sets the residence time tau = V/Q, which together with the kineti… |

## `cstr`  (cstr operation)

Operation block of a continuous stirred-tank reactor.  Reactor volume is the only sizing knob; T is taken from the feed stream.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `V_R` | ✓ | number | m^3 | Total liquid volume held in the CSTR. |

## `cyclone`  (cyclone operation)

Gas-solid cyclone separator.  Hardware: the body diameter (and effective number of turns).  The cut diameter d50, the grade-efficiency curve η(d), the collection efficiency and the pressure drop ΔP are RESULTS.  The collection correlation is a SELECTABLE sub-model (model keyword) -- simple data-anchored correlations, swap to compare.  Needs a particle-size distribution on the feed (solids block) and a transport model for the gas viscosity.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `bodyDiameter` | ✓ | number | m | Cyclone body diameter [m].  Sets the inlet area (D_c^2/8) and width (D_c/4) of the Lapple standard geometry. |
| `numberOfTurns` |   | number | - | Effective number of gas revolutions (typical ~5).  More turns -> smaller d50 -> finer cut. |
| `C` |   | number | - | Geometric-configuration constant for the LeithLicht model (default 402.88, calibratable). |
| `beta` |   | number | - | Grade-efficiency slope for IoziaLeith (default 3.5), Barth (default 4) and Muschelknautz internal (default 3).  Lapple is the β=2 special… |
| `alpha` |   | number | - | Tangential-velocity factor v_θ = α·v_i on the control surface (Barth, default 1.5, calibratable). |
| `heightFactor` |   | number | - | Control-surface height H_cs/D_c for the Barth model (default 2, calibratable). |
| `cLimit` |   | number | - | Limit solids loading c_lim [kg solid/kg gas] for the Muschelknautz mass-loading effect (default 0.05, calibratable).  Above it, surplus s… |

## `dewT`  (dewT operation)

Dew-point temperature at fixed pressure: find the T at which the first liquid droplet condenses from a saturated vapour of the given composition.  Solved by Newton-1D in T on Sum_i y_i / K_i(T) = 1.  Hardware-only: specify the pressure P; the dew temperature is the RESULT.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `P` | ✓ | number | Pa | System pressure at which the dew point is evaluated. |

## `distillationColumn`  (distillationColumn operation)

Operation block of a distillation column with constant molar overflow.  Feed enters at feedStage; condenser is stage 0, reboiler is stage nStages+1.  Solved by the sequential bubble-point (Wang-Henke, default) or the rigorous simultaneous MESH Newton (stable on azeotropes).

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `nStages` | ✓ | integer | — | Number of equilibrium stages between condenser and reboiler (exclusive).  Total stages = nStages + 2. |
| `feedStage` | ✓ | integer | — | 1-indexed stage where the feed enters (1 = top tray below condenser).  Must satisfy 1 <= feedStage <= nStages. |
| `refluxRatio` | ✓ | number | — | L / D, the ratio of liquid returned to the column over distillate withdrawn. |
| `distillateRate` | ✓ | number | kmol/h | Top product molar flow rate. |
| `P` |   | number | bar | Column pressure (assumed constant across all stages).  Falls back to the feed-stream pressure if omitted. |
| `feedQuality` |   | number | — | Convention q = 1 saturated liquid, q = 0 saturated vapor.  Defaults to 1 (saturated-liquid feed). |

**Intrinsic heat ports (`condenser` / `reboiler`).**  A distillation column
(and `shortcutColumn`) exposes two heat ports that need NO `energyOutputs`
block — the port value IS the column's reported duty KPI
(`Q_condenser_kW` / `Q_reboiler_kW`).  Another unit consumes that heat with
an ordinary consumer-side `energyInputs` entry referring to `<col>.condenser`
or `<col>.reboiler`, e.g. a downstream `heater` driven by the column's
rejected condenser duty:

```
energyInputs
(
    { from column01.condenser;  kind heat;  target Q; }
);
```

The condenser rejects heat (heats the consumer); the reboiler demands heat
(cools whoever supplies it).  `kind` MUST be `heat`.  **Forward links only:**
the column must be listed (and solve) BEFORE its heat consumer — a feedback
link (e.g. the condenser preheating the column's own feed) is an energy tear,
not yet supported.  See `docs/ai/energy.md` and tutorial
`heatlink01_condenser_to_heater`.

## `electricLoad`  (electricLoad operation)

Shaft sink / electrical generator.  Hardware-free apart from the generator
efficiency `eta`; it has no stream ports.  The shaft power `W_shaft` is NOT
authored on the operation block — it arrives over an energy wire from a
turbine (or any work producer) via `energyInputs ( { from <unit>.shaft; kind
work; target W_shaft; } )`.  The electrical power shipped to the grid
(`W_electric = eta * W_shaft`) and the dissipated losses (`(1-eta)*W_shaft`)
are RESULTS.  See `docs/ai/energy.md`; tutorials `rankine01_water`,
`combined01_brayton_rankine`, `proxy01_gas_loop`.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `eta` |   | number | - | Generator efficiency (shaft → electrical).  0 < eta <= 1; default 1.0.  The (1-eta) fraction is dissipated as heat (the W_losses KPI). |

## `evaporator`  (evaporator operation)

Single-effect evaporator modelled as PHYSICAL EQUIPMENT.  Two inlets (feed liquid + heating steam) and three outlets (concentrated liquid, solvent vapour, condensate).  The unit op accepts only physical parameters --- heat-exchange area, overall HTC, operating pressure --- and computes V/F, duty Q, T_boil (with BPE = K_b * m_solute), and steam demand from the energy balance Q = U.A.dT.  External targets such as 'V/F = 0.5' or 'x_outlet = 0.25' are NOT operation parameters here; they belong on an outer DesignSpec controller.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `P` | ✓ | number | Pa | Vapour-side pressure of the evaporator.  Sets the solvent's saturation temperature; the actual boiling point is T_sat + K_b * m_solute (B… |
| `area` | ✓ | number | m^2 | Physical heat-transfer surface area of the tube bundle / heating jacket. |
| `U` | ✓ | number | W/(m^2.K) | Overall HTC across the heating-side film + tube wall + boiling-side film.  Typical values: clean steam-water 2000-3000, scaled or low-pre… |
| `Tref` |   | number | K | Reference for the sensible-heat integrals.  Only differences enter the balance. |

## `fitParameters`  (fitParameters operation)

Levenberg-Marquardt regression of property-package parameters against experimental data.  Each parameter is identified by a dot-path into the loaded propertyDict with `initial` / `min` / `max`; the residual block selects what to fit against (e.g. T_bubble vs x at a given P) and supplies the experimental points.  Outputs two CSVs: a fit log (chi^2 + parameter trajectory per iteration) and a parity CSV (model vs experiment at the optimum).  The PropsView renders the fit log as a chi^2 + parameter-trajectory plot, and the parity CSV as a scatter against the y=x line.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `parameters` | ✓ | array[object] | — | Each entry identifies a scalar in the loaded propertyDict to vary, with bounds and an initial guess.  `path` is dot-separated (e.g. `activityMo… |
| `residual` | ✓ | object | — | What to fit against (e.g. T_bubble at a fixed P) + the experimental data points. |
| `options` |   | object | — | Tuning knobs for the Levenberg-Marquardt loop (lambda, tolerance, maxIter).  Defaults are fine for most cases. |

## `gasSolidSplitter`  (gasSolidSplitter operation)

Ideal, specification-driven gas-solid separator -- the simple sibling of the cyclone / bag filter.  You SET the split (no efficiency physics, no size classification); the feed PSD passes through unchanged.  Use it when a physics model is overkill, as a placeholder, or to bound a design.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `solidsRecovery` |   | number | - | Fraction of the solid mass sent to capturedSolids (default 1.0 = perfect separation). |
| `gasCarryover` |   | number | - | Fraction of the gas leaving WITH the solids outlet (default 0.0 = no gas in the solids stream). |

## `gibbsReactor`  (gibbsReactor operation)

Gibbs reactor: equilibrium composition by minimising the total Gibbs free energy of the species mixture, subject to atom-balance constraints (element conservation).  Lagrangian formulation, least-squares-seeded NewtonND, three selectable `model` paths: elementPotential (gas / 1 ideal condensable), reactiveFlash (multi-condensable + NRTL), directMin (multi-start Nelder-Mead).  The user lists the atomic elements considered + the candidate species with their atom counts; the simulator finds the equilibrium mixture.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `T` | ✓ | number | K | Temperature at which the equilibrium is evaluated.  For an adiabatic Gibbs reactor (energy balance), wrap the unit in a DesignSpec that v… |
| `P` | ✓ | number | Pa | Pressure at which the equilibrium is evaluated.  Enters via the ideal-gas chemical-potential term mu = mu_pure(T) + R T ln(y_i P / 1 bar). |
| `elements` | ✓ | array[string] | — | Chemical elements considered in the atom-balance constraints (one constraint per element).  Each species declares its atom counts in this… |
| `species` | ✓ | array[object] | — | Species present in the equilibrium mixture, each with its atom counts (one number per element listed above). |

## `heatExchanger`  (heatExchanger operation)

Two-stream heat exchanger solved by the effectiveness-NTU method (the rating problem).  Hardware-only: the heat-transfer area, the overall coefficient U, and an optional flow arrangement.  The outlet temperatures, the duty Q and the LMTD are RESULTS.  For 'I need T_hot_out = X' wrap the unit in a DesignSpec outerDict that manipulates $area (or $U) with a target on this unit's T_hot_out KPI.  This is the TWO-stream unit; the single-stream `heater` (furnished duty Q) is separate.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `area` | ✓ | number | m2 | The exchanger surface.  Larger area -> more NTU -> the outlet temperatures approach the thermodynamic limit. |
| `U` | ✓ | number | W/m2/K | Overall U for the surface (film + wall + fouling lumped).  NTU = U*area / C_min. |
| `flow` |   | string | — | counter (default, more effective) or co (co-current). |

## `heater`  (heater operation)

Operation block of a credo-pure single-stream heater.  Hardware-only: specify the absolute thermal power Q (W or kW; positive = heating, negative = cooling).  The outlet temperature T_out is a RESULT of the enthalpy balance, not a spec.  For 'I need T_out = X' wrap the unit in a DesignSpec outerDict that manipulates $Q with a target on this unit's T_out KPI.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `Q` | ✓ | number | W | Absolute thermal power delivered to the stream.  Positive heats, negative cools.  Think of it as the electrical rating of the heater or t… |
| `Tref` |   | number | K | Reference temperature for the enthalpy integrals.  Only differences enter the balance. |

## `isothermalFlash`  (isothermalFlash operation)

Operation block of an isothermal two-phase flash.  Both T and P are optional overrides of the feed-stream conditions; leaving them out runs the flash at the feed T and P (natural propagation through a flowsheet).

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `T` |   | number | K | Override the feed temperature.  If omitted, the flash inherits the feed T. |
| `P` |   | number | bar | Override the feed pressure.  If omitted, the flash inherits the feed P. |

## `mixer`  (mixer operation)

Adiabatic stream mixer: sums the component molar flows of all inlets and closes an adiabatic energy balance (Newton-1D in T_out on Sum H_in = H_out) to find the outlet temperature.  Hardware-free -- the mixer has no operating parameters; its behaviour is entirely fixed by the inlet streams.

*(no operation-block fields)*

## `pfr`  (pfr operation)

Operation block of an isothermal plug-flow reactor.  The volume is integrated by hand-rolled RK4; nSteps controls the spatial discretisation.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `V_R` | ✓ | number | m^3 | Total reactor volume.  RK4 integrates along the residence time interval [0, V_R / Q_feed]. |
| `nSteps` |   | integer | — | Number of integration steps along the reactor length.  100 is plenty for first-order kinetics; raise it if the rate is stiff. |
| `writeInterval` |   | integer | — | How often to print the (V, T, conversion) profile along the reactor.  0 = silent end-point only. |

## `propertyPoint`  (propertyPoint operation)

Single-point property evaluation: pick a (T, P, composition) and the thermo package reports every property the chosen models can compute (Psat, gamma_i, K_i, Cp_ig, H_ig, S_ig, Z, v, H_real, S_real,...).  No CSV output -- results print to the run log; open the Log tab in the output panel to read them.  Useful for JANAF/SRK/PR audit points and as a quick property check.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `state` | ✓ | object | — | The (T, P, composition) at which the property is evaluated. |

## `propertyScan1D`  (propertyScan1D operation)

One-dimensional property sweep: vary ONE state variable (T, P, or a mole fraction) across n evenly-spaced points, evaluating the requested properties at each.  Writes a long-form CSV (one row per scan point) ready for plotting -- the PropsView's CsvAutoPlot will render it as a multi-line plot, one trace per property.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `vary` | ✓ | object | — | Which variable to sweep, between which limits, and how many points (linearly spaced). |
| `state` |   | object | — | The (T, P, composition) values held fixed -- whichever the `vary.variable` is NOT.  Optional when vary covers the only axis you care about. |
| `properties` | ✓ | array[string] | — | Per-point properties to compute and write to the CSV (Psat_<comp>, T_bubble, gamma_<comp>, Cp_ig, H_ig, S_ig, Z, v_molar, H_R, S_R, H_rea… |
| `output` | ✓ | object | — |  |

## `propertyScan2D`  (propertyScan2D operation)

Two-dimensional property sweep: vary two state variables on a regular grid, evaluating each property at every (x, y) node.  Writes a long-form CSV (one row per node) ready to be pivoted into a heatmap -- the PropsView's CsvAutoPlot renders it that way with a property selector.  NaN cells are written when a property evaluation fails at a corner (e.g. an unphysical T_bubble at low P) and the loop continues.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `varyX` | ✓ | object | — |  |
| `varyY` | ✓ | object | — |  |
| `state` |   | object | — | The state values held fixed across the grid -- typically just the composition. |
| `properties` | ✓ | array[string] | — |  |
| `output` | ✓ | object | — |  |

## `pump`  (pump operation)

Operation block of a credo-pure incompressible-liquid pump.  Hardware-only: specify the shaft power W_shaft (W or kW, positive) and the pump efficiency eta.  The discharge pressure P_out (P_in + eta*W_shaft/(F*v_molar)) and the small dissipation temperature rise are RESULTS.  For 'pump to X bar' wrap the unit in a DesignSpec that manipulates $W_shaft with a target on this unit's P_out KPI (converges in one step --- the relation is linear).

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `W_shaft` | ✓ | number | W | Mechanical power delivered to the liquid (motor rating).  Positive. |
| `eta` | ✓ | number | - | Hydraulic + mechanical efficiency: useful v*dP work / shaft work.  0 < eta <= 1.  The (1-eta) fraction heats the liquid (a small dT). |

## `shortcutColumn`  (shortcutColumn operation)

Preliminary column design by the Fenske-Underwood-Gilliland (FUG) shortcut method.  Specify the two key components and their recoveries to the distillate, plus the operating reflux (refluxRatio directly, or refluxFactor = R/R_min).  Returns N_min (Fenske), R_min (Underwood), the actual stage count N (Gilliland) and the feed stage (Kirkbride).  Assumes constant relative volatility -- not for azeotropes; use the rigorous distillationColumn for those.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `lightKey` | ✓ | string | — | The more-volatile key component, sharp at the top. |
| `heavyKey` | ✓ | string | — | The less-volatile key component, sharp at the bottom. |
| `recoveryLK` | ✓ | number | - | Fraction of the feed light key that leaves in the distillate (e.g. 0.99). |
| `recoveryHK` | ✓ | number | - | Fraction of the feed heavy key that leaves in the distillate (e.g. 0.01). |
| `refluxRatio` |   | number | - | Reflux ratio R directly; must exceed R_min.  Use this OR refluxFactor. |
| `refluxFactor` |   | number | - | Operating reflux as a multiple of the minimum (e.g. 1.3).  Use this OR refluxRatio. |

## `solidDryer`  (solidDryer operation)

Contact / through-circulation solid dryer.  One inlet (a wet solid: crystals in s[] carrying bound moisture) and two outlets (dry solid + vapour).  The unit dries the solid toward the EQUILIBRIUM moisture set by the air's relative humidity and the solid's GAB sorption isotherm (a sample-specific `sorption {}` block on the component.dat).  X_final = min(X_in, X_eq); the water removed, the duty and the equilibrium moisture are RESULTS.  Complements the spray dryer: spray = atomise a liquid into powder; solidDryer = polish an already-solid powder down to spec.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `airTemperature` | ✓ | number | K | Temperature of the drying air.  Hotter air lowers the equilibrium moisture the solid can reach (the isotherm shifts). |
| `relativeHumidity` |   | number | - | Relative humidity (water activity) of the drying air, 0-1.  The solid dries until its surface water activity equals this; the GAB isother… |

## `splitter`  (splitter operation)

Stream splitter: divides one inlet into several outlets by the user-supplied split fractions (which must sum to 1).  All branches share the same temperature, pressure and composition as the inlet -- this is a flow split, NOT a separation (contrast the gasSolidSplitter, which spec-splits solids but likewise does not classify by size).

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `fractions` | ✓ | array[number] | — | Per-outlet split fraction (one entry per output stream).  Must sum to 1; each entry in [0, 1].  The ordering matches the `outputs` list. |

## `sprayDryer`  (sprayDryer operation)

Single-stage rotary-atomiser spray dryer (PHASE 1).  Two inlets (feed liquid + drying air) and two outlets (powder + exhaust air).  The operation block carries only atomiser HARDWARE; the exhaust temperature, droplet/particle sizes, drying coefficients (h, k_c) and the constant-rate drying time are RESULTS computed from the energy balance, the Friedman rotary-disc correlation and Ranz-Marshall transfer.  Needs a transport block (viscosity + thermalConductivity + diffusivity) in the propertyDict for the drying kinetics.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `wheelDiameter` | ✓ | number | m | Diameter of the spinning atomiser wheel.  With the feed rate it sets the liquid loading per unit wetted perimeter, hence the droplet size. |
| `wheelSpeed` |   | number | rpm | Rotational speed of the atomiser wheel.  Faster spinning -> smaller droplets (d32 ~ N^-0.6 in the Friedman correlation).  Industrial disc… |
| `liquidViscosity` |   | number | Pa.s | Dynamic viscosity of the feed liquid, used in the atomisation correlation.  Defaults to water (1e-3 Pa.s) -- Choupo does not yet carry li… |
| `surfaceTension` |   | number | N/m | Surface tension of the feed liquid, used in the atomisation correlation.  Defaults to water (0.072 N/m). |
| `spreadParameter` |   | number | - | Width of the powder particle-size distribution.  The droplet sizes follow a Rosin-Rammler Q(d)=1-exp(-(d/d')^n) around d32; larger n = na… |
| `chamberDiameter` |   | number | m | Diameter of the drying chamber (HARDWARE).  Sets the gas superficial velocity v_gas = Q_gas/(pi D^2/4), hence the particle fall velocity … |
| `chamberHeight` |   | number | m | Height the particle falls through (HARDWARE).  Residence time = chamberHeight / particle fall velocity; with a sorption isotherm on the s… |
| `flow` |   | string | — | co-current (gas and droplets fall together, v_particle = v_gas + v_terminal) or counter-current (v_particle = v_terminal - v_gas).  Co-cu… |

## `steamTables`  (steamTables operation — choupoProps)

IAPWS-IF97 industrial water/steam properties (a `propsDict` operation under
**choupoProps**, like `propertyPoint` — not a flowsheet unit).  At startup it
self-checks the transcribed coefficients against the release verification
tables and REFUSES the run on any deviation > 1e-8.  Give exactly ONE mode
block per operation:

```
{ name pt1;     type steamTables;  point      { P 3 MPa;  T 300 K; } }
{ name satline; type steamTables;  saturation { from 0.01 degC; to 350 degC; n 50; }
                                   output     { file saturation.csv; } }
{ name iso1bar; type steamTables;  isobar     { P 1 bar; from 20 degC; to 300 degC; n 57; }
                                   output     { file isobar_1bar.csv; } }
```

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `point` | one-of | dict | — | `{ P ...; T ...; }` — one (p,T) state: v, rho, h, u, s, cp, cv, w + the IF97 region, printed and stored as diags (psat at T as a free verification pin).  `output { file ...; }` optional (one-row CSV). |
| `saturation` | one-of | dict | — | `{ from ...; to ...; n ...; }` — T-scan of the saturation line: psat + the saturated f/g pairs (v, h, s) + h_fg per row.  Valid 273.15–623.15 K only (above that the liquid side is region 3 — refused loudly).  `output { file ...; }` required. |
| `isobar` | one-of | dict | — | `{ P ...; from ...; to ...; n ...; }` — h, s, v, cp vs T at fixed P.  A subcritical isobar's saturation crossing is ANNOUNCED before the scan (expect the h/s/v jump).  Regions 3/5 refused loudly.  `output { file ...; }` required. |

CSV columns are canonical SI (h, u, s, cp, cv in J/kg-based units).  Tutorial:
`tutorials/props/steam/steam01_if97_verification` (locks every release
verification table as golden KPIs at reltol 1e-8).

## `stripper`  (stripper operation)

Counter-current multistage gas stripper (Kremser) -- the inverse of the absorber.  A rich liquid (top) is contacted with a lean stripping gas (bottom) to drive the solute OUT of the liquid.  Hardware-only: the number of theoretical stages.  The per-solute removal is a RESULT; the key group is the stripping factor S = K*V/L.  For 'I need 95 % removal' wrap the unit in a DesignSpec on $stages.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `stages` | ✓ | number | - | Number of equilibrium stages = the column height.  More stages give more stripping (the Kremser chart). |

## `turbine`  (turbine operation)

Operation block of a credo-pure gas-phase turbine (expander).  Hardware-only: specify the shaft power W_shaft (W or kW, NEGATIVE because work is extracted) and the isentropic efficiency eta.  The discharge pressure P_out, the discharge temperature T_out and the expansion ratio are RESULTS.  For 'I need to exhaust at X bar' wrap the unit in a DesignSpec that manipulates $W_shaft with a target on this unit's P_out KPI.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `W_shaft` | ✓ | number | W | Mechanical power extracted from the gas.  NEGATIVE (work leaves the fluid).  Use a compressor block for compression. |
| `eta` | ✓ | number | - | Machine characteristic: w_real / w_isentropic.  0 < eta <= 1.  Lower eta means less of the available expansion work is captured (the rest… |

## `valve`  (valve operation)

Isenthalpic throttle (Joule-Thomson let-down).  Hardware-only: the single knob
is the downstream pressure `P`; a valve only DROPS pressure (a restriction, not
a machine — to raise pressure use a `pump` for liquid or a `compressor` for
gas).  W = 0 and Q = 0 by definition: the expansion is isenthalpic, so the
outlet temperature T_out, the vapour fraction vf and the regime are RESULTS of
an outer Newton on H_out = H_in at the new pressure (a hot liquid let down can
flash).  One combined outlet (phases NOT separated — chain a `flash` / drum
downstream to split them).  Tutorial `credo01_valve_heater_drum`.

| Field | Required | Type | Unit | Description |
|---|:-:|---|---|---|
| `P` | ✓ | number | bar | Downstream (outlet) pressure.  Must be below the inlet pressure — a valve can only drop pressure.  T_out and vf are results of the isenthalpic balance. |
